description: A rain-soaked waystation on the Reach Circuit — two wary locals, a missing senior Cape, and a wardstone that hasn't been right since it rang like a cracked bell four months ago.
customIntroduction: Rain off the coach road, and then warmth: tallow, wet wool, a low ceiling. The big man behind the bar works a rag over a cup he's already polished twice. In the corner a local watches you over her mug. Your cape is still stiff with road-dust — and you're the first Cape these people have seen in a long while. They know it before you speak. Your writ is plain: walk the Circuit, recertify the wardstones. And learn why Senior Cape Edran Vael has sent no word in three seasons.
customContext: INTRODUCTORY SOCIAL ENCOUNTER — no combat. First scene; teach dialogue and ability checks. Reed is a freshly licensed Cape-mage (treat as a Wizard): bookish, polite, theory-rich and field-green. Two neutral NPCs — Bram the keeper and a local villager — must NOT be made hostile. Give campaign basics freely; gate richer detail behind Persuasion/Insight/History/Arcana DC 10–13. Reed must leave with ONE concrete lead: the north-wood wardstone has cracked "like a struck bell." The two conversation trees own their NPCs' dialogue — the villager carries the wardstone ring, the two riders, and that Vael "looked worried" — so only voice an NPC yourself if the player picks the tree's free-talk choice. Reward good questions and let her ask freely.
objective: Speak with Bram Holdfast the innkeeper and the local villager to learn what you can about Senior Cape Edran Vael and the state of the wardstones on the Reach Circuit. Leave with at least one concrete lead.

then.message: The intro is on screen — the warm waystation, Bram at the bar, a villager in the corner, both clocking Reed's white cape. Don't re-describe it. Pick up from the room's quiet attention and invite Reed to make the first move.

Reed has her lead: the north-wood wardstone, cracked "like a struck bell." Let the weight land in a sentence or two — the villager's unease about Vael, the dawning sense that "routine work" is anything but. She can keep asking, but point her toward the door when she's ready. Do NOT narrate her stepping outside — that fires when she actually leaves.
You have your lead. When you're ready, head south to the door and step out into the rain — the Circuit begins on the north road.

then.text: You step back out into the rain with a name in your notes and a cracked wardstone on your map. The Reach Circuit begins here.
